title: Audit agent memories for undocumented decisions and add to docs/decisions/
description:
## Goal

Ensure all project-specific design decisions in agent memories have counterparts in `docs/decisions/`. Gap-fill only — no reorganization of existing docs.

## Process

### First pass: create decisions index

Docs-api agent reads all `docs/decisions/*.md` and creates `docs/decisions/INDEX.md` — one line per doc (title + one-sentence summary). This index is the lightweight reference for subsequent passes.

### Per-agent pass: compare memory vs index

For each of the 8 agent memories (`.claude/agent-memory/*/MEMORY.md`), compare against the index. Identify:
- **Gaps**: project-specific design decisions in memory with no counterpart in `docs/decisions/`
- **Collisions**: memory content that contradicts an existing decision doc

Skip: common coding conventions (visibility modifiers, naming patterns, file lists, changelog entries). Only track "we chose X over Y because Z" decisions.

### Gap resolution

Docs agent reports all gaps and collisions to orchestrator. Orchestrator triages with user, then docs-api writes missing decision docs.

## Communication flow

Docs agent → orchestrator → user. No direct agent-user interaction. Docs agent flags anything uncertain as a doubt.

## Convention to add

Two locations:
1. **AGENTS.md**: brief rule — "project-specific decisions go in `docs/decisions/`, not just agent memory"
2. **`docs/dev/release-checklist.md`**: full process — "biweekly memory audit at each minor version bump"

## Scope

- 8 agent memories (build-tooling, cli-features, daemon-core, docs-api, hook-ipc, test-writer, tui-rendering, tui-visual)
- 35 existing decision docs in `docs/decisions/`
- Gap-fill only, no reorganization
- If organizational problems found, log as separate findings (future issue)
close_reason: Audited 8 agent memories, fixed 2 collisions, wrote 4 new decision docs, added INDEX.md, release checklist, and AGENTS.md convention. Commit: ee5fbda

title: Protect main branch and require PRs for CI
description: Stop running CI on every push to main. Protect main branch so even administrators cannot push directly — all changes must go through PRs. This prevents unnecessary CI runs and enforces review workflow. Steps: 1) Add branch protection rules on GitHub (require PR, no direct push, include administrators), 2) Update CI workflow to only run on pull_request events (not push to main), 3) Optionally keep push-triggered CI for other branches.
close_reason: Branch protection configured: required CI checks (5 jobs), required PRs (0 approvals), enforce_admins=false for owner bypass, no force pushes or deletions.

title: Verify acd binary is in PATH during install
description: Hook commands use bare 'acd' — if not in PATH, hooks fail silently. The install command should check that 'acd' resolves in PATH and suggest 'cargo install --path crates/agent-console-dashboard' if not found. See docs/end-to-end-blockers.md B2.
close_reason: Closed

title: Install command: write hooks to ~/.claude/settings.json
description: Plugin is not installed in Claude Code. .claude-plugin/ is a gitignored build artifact — Claude Code doesn't auto-discover it. Add 'acd install' command that writes hooks directly to ~/.claude/settings.json (user scope). Also add 'acd uninstall' to remove them. See docs/end-to-end-blockers.md B1+B6.
close_reason: Closed

title: Rename auto-start to lazy-start in codebase
description: Rename all auto_start/auto-start references to lazy_start/lazy-start for consistency with the new design. Includes: connect_with_auto_start() -> connect_with_lazy_start(), comments, module docs, error messages. Related to acd-tz3 (wire lazy-start into hook handler).
close_reason: renamed all auto-start references to lazy-start across 4 files

title: Wire lazy-start into claude-hook handler
description: run_claude_hook_command() uses raw UnixStream::connect() via run_set_command(), bypassing the existing connect_with_auto_start() in client/connection.rs. The auto-start infrastructure (spawn daemon + exponential backoff retry) is built but not wired into the hook path. Fix: make the hook handler use connect_with_auto_start() so the daemon starts automatically on first hook trigger.
close_reason: Wired connect_with_lazy_start() into run_claude_hook_async(), daemon now auto-starts on first hook trigger

title: Update E002 session code: verify session_id from JSON stdin
description: Verify session management code uses session_id from JSON stdin (not basename PWD). Check any hook script examples or test fixtures that use the old pattern. Scope: crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/ session-related code. See docs/architecture/2026-01-31-decision-amendments.md and Q16.
close_reason: Verified: all hook scripts parse session_id from JSON stdin. Fixed stale $SESSION_ID $ARGS placeholders in daemon/mod.rs command templates. No code uses basename PWD.

title: Update E009/E011 integration: daemon fetches usage, not TUI
description: Code changes needed: TUI should no longer call claude_usage::get_usage() directly. Instead, daemon fetches every 3 minutes and broadcasts via subscription. Update any TUI code that imports claude-usage crate directly. Scope: crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/. See docs/architecture/2026-01-31-discussion-decisions.md D3-D4.
close_reason: UsageFetcher wired into daemon, SUB extended with USAGE messages, TUI receives and stores usage data

title: Update E001 daemon code: auto-stop 60min + actor model
description: Code changes needed after epic amendments: (1) Update AUTO_STOP_IDLE_THRESHOLD_SECS from 1800 to 3600 in daemon code, (2) Refactor connection handling from tokio::spawn per connection to single-threaded actor model with mpsc queue, (3) Replace Arc<RwLock<HashMap>> with plain HashMap. Scope: crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/daemon/. See docs/architecture/2026-01-31-decision-amendments.md amendments 1-2.
close_reason: Split into focused issues: acd-2co (P1, idle timer implementation), acd-jnd (P4, configurability), acd-51l (P4, actor model deferred). Code audit found no deadlocks possible, one inconsequential TOCTOU. Current RwLock model is idiomatic Rust and correct for our scale.

title: Deep content audit of all 55 story files
description:
## Context

Structural audit completed on 2026-01-30 (see docs/audits/2026-01-30-story-structural-audit.md).
All files pass structural validation (docs/docs-scripts/validate-stories.sh).
This task covers the CONTENT quality review that was not yet done.

## Branch

Continue on: docs/create-missing-stories

## What to Check Per Story File

For each of the 55 story files in docs/stories/:

1. **Description quality**: Clear user story format ("As a..., I want..., So that...")
2. **Context section**: Explains WHY this story exists, not just WHAT
3. **Implementation Details accuracy**: Files to modify/create match actual codebase structure
   - Check paths like `src/daemon/server.rs` vs `crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/daemon/server.rs`
   - Flag any paths that reference non-existent modules
4. **Acceptance criteria**: Testable, specific, not vague
5. **Dependencies**: Cross-references to other stories are accurate (correct story IDs, correct relationship)
6. **Scope consistency with epic**: Story scope matches what the parent epic describes
7. **Points estimation**: Reasonable relative to other stories (1=trivial, 2=small, 3=medium, 5=large)
8. **Code examples**: If present, check they use correct crate/module names
9. **Out of scope**: Items listed make sense and aren't things that should be in scope

## Approach

1. Read stories in epic order (E001 stories first, then E002, etc.)
2. Read the parent epic first to understand goals
3. Review each story against the checklist above
4. Record findings per file in docs/audits/2026-01-30-story-content-audit.md
5. Fix issues found
6. Commit fixes

## Already Verified (Do NOT Re-check)

- File existence (all present)
- Header format (Story ID, Epic link, Status, Priority, Points)
- Required sections present (Description, Acceptance Criteria)
- Epic links resolve to correct files
- Epic story tables have hyperlinks
- Sprint-status.yaml alignment
- Story ID matches filename

## Key Files

- Validation script: docs/docs-scripts/validate-stories.sh
- Structural audit: docs/audits/2026-01-30-story-structural-audit.md
- Sprint status: planning-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml
close_reason: Content audit complete. First pass fixed 37 files (paths, E009 rewrite, stale refs). BMAD second pass scored 94% — 49/55 stories implementation-ready, 4 medium + 6 minor AC specificity issues remain.

title: Prevent XSS: never use innerHTML/dangerouslySetInnerHTML
description: htm auto-escapes by default, but payload rendering (JSON viewer, event detail) must never bypass this. Add explicit rule in AGENTS.md: no innerHTML, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML. All payload rendering goes through htm's tagged template escaping.

title: Enforce parameterized SQL queries — no string concatenation
description: All SQLite queries must use parameterized queries (db.prepare().run()). Never concatenate user-provided values into SQL strings. Add this as an explicit rule in AGENTS.md. Prevents SQL injection via hook stdin payloads.

title: Architectural decision: persistent backend daemon (auto-started by handler)
description: hookwatch needs a persistent Bun daemon process. Handler auto-starts it on first hook invocation if not running. Daemon manages: SQLite writes, web UI server, WebSocket broadcast, wrap CLI. Self-terminates after inactivity. This resolves cold start performance (NFR-1), hook-server communication, and multi-plugin shared state. Update design doc with this decision.
notes: Idle timeout: daemon self-terminates after ~30-60 min of inactivity (no hook events, no web UI connections). Next hook invocation restarts it. No data loss — SQLite is the source of truth, not in-memory state. Use WAL mode for concurrent access.

title: Two-plugin architecture: hooks plugin + skills plugin with shared backend
description: Split hookwatch into two Claude Code plugins sharing one backend. Plugin 1 (hooks, v0): event capture via handler.ts. Plugin 2 (skills, v1): agent-queryable skills. Both use the shared Bun backend (SQLite + web UI + wrap CLI). Reason: plugin system doesn't support partial installation. Design doc needs updating.

title: Phase 4: Release
description: Release preparation for v0.1.0. GitHub remote setup, packaging, docs finalization, version tagging, installation verification.

title: Phase 3: QA & Hardening
description: Multi-round quality assurance after implementation. Integration testing, performance validation (NFR-1: <100ms), edge cases, dogfooding hookwatch on itself.

title: Phase 2: Implementation
description: Orchestrated execution of v0 stories. Each story includes its own unit tests. Implementation follows the story order from Phase 1.

title: Phase 1: Design & PRD
description: Use BMAD plugin to produce PRD, break v0 scope into epics and stories. All design artifacts finalized before implementation begins.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: product brief, PRD, architecture, and epics/stories all done

title: Write hookwatch design doc (goals, non-goals, FR, NFR)
description: Informed by observability repo analysis. Cover: goals, non-goals, functional requirements, non-functional requirements. Draw clear lines about what hookwatch does and doesn't do.
close_reason: All 5 open questions resolved. Design doc complete with 102 features classified, 18 events confirmed, key decisions locked.

title: Pin Rust toolchain version to fix recurring CI rustfmt mismatch
description:
## Problem

CI lint job (`cargo fmt --all -- --check`) fails intermittently because local
rustfmt and CI rustfmt format code differently. Root cause: Homebrew installs
Rust 1.93.0 (rustfmt 1.8.0) but CI's `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable` installs
1.93.1 (latest stable). Patch version differences cause different line-wrapping
decisions in rustfmt.

This has occurred twice:
1. Session where we upgraded from 1.89.0 → 1.93.0 and reformatted everything
2. Now: agent-written code formatted with 1.93.0 fails CI's 1.93.1 check

## Fix

Add `rust-toolchain.toml` to repo root to pin the toolchain version:

```toml
[toolchain]
channel = "1.93.0"
components = ["rustfmt", "clippy"]
```

This ensures:
- Local dev uses the pinned version (rustup reads this file automatically)
- CI uses the same version (dtolnay/rust-toolchain respects rust-toolchain.toml)
- No more version drift between local and CI
- Version upgrades become deliberate (bump the file, reformat, commit)

Also reformat the 2 files that currently differ (cli.rs, rendering.rs).

## Files

- New: `rust-toolchain.toml` (repo root)
- Fix: `crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/cli_tests/cli.rs`
- Fix: `crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/tui/views/dashboard/tests/rendering.rs`
- Optional: update CI to remove explicit `components: rustfmt, clippy` since
  rust-toolchain.toml handles it
close_reason: Pinned via rust-toolchain.toml + fixed flaky test

title: Pre-commit hook: reduce verbose test output
description:
cargo test --workspace --quiet produces 81 lines in the pre-commit hook. The --quiet flag reduces cargo build output but the test harness still prints individual test dots (625+ dots), which is the main noise source.

What to keep: warnings (unused imports etc.), 'running N tests' headers, 'test result:' summaries, 'error:' messages.
What to remove: individual test dots (the '.....' lines).

Fix approach: in scripts/pre-commit.sh, capture test output and filter out dot-only lines. On test failure, show full unfiltered output. On success, show only warnings, headers, and summary lines.

There is no cargo flag to suppress dots while keeping warnings — the test harness only offers terse (dots), pretty (names), or json (nightly) formats. This must be solved at the shell script level.

Current hook command: cargo test --workspace --quiet -- --test-threads=4
File to modify: scripts/pre-commit.sh (lines 49-50)
close_reason: Pre-commit hook now filters dot/i progress lines. Output reduced from 81 to ~62 lines (all meaningful content preserved).

title: Decide plugin.json vs settings.json hook strategy
description: plugin.json embeds hooks inline (valid per docs) but becomes irrelevant if install command writes to ~/.claude/settings.json. Decision: keep plugin.json for distribution AND settings.json for personal use? Or drop the plugin approach? See docs/end-to-end-blockers.md B4.
close_reason: Decision: keep both. plugin.json (build.rs) for future distribution via marketplace. settings.json (acd install) for personal/immediate use. They serve different scopes and don't conflict.

title: Add missing hook events: SessionEnd, Notification
description: Only 3 of 14 hook events are registered. Missing critical events: SessionEnd → closed (sessions never close), Notification/elicitation_dialog → question (AskUserQuestion), Notification/permission_prompt → attention. Must also verify Status enum accepts 'closed' and 'question' via claude-hook subcommand. See docs/end-to-end-blockers.md B3.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement auto-stop idle timer (60min constant)
description: Add AUTO_STOP_IDLE_SECS = 3600 constant to daemon. Daemon should auto-stop when no sessions are active for 60 minutes. Logic lives in the main event loop (reset on any session activity, shut down when timer fires). Split from acd-ojb which bundled this with actor model refactor. Configurability deferred to acd-jnd (P4).
close_reason: Implemented: idle auto-stop timer with AUTO_STOP_IDLE_SECS=3600 constant, merged to master

title: Fix claude-hooks to use correct Claude Code hooks format
description: The claude-hooks library uses wrong format. Expected object-based format with event keys: { "hooks": { "Stop": [{ "hooks": [...] }] } } but library uses array format { "hooks": [...] }. Need to update settings.rs and related code to handle actual Claude Code schema.
close_reason: Fixed claude-hooks library to use correct Claude Code hooks format (object-based instead of array-based). Updated all test files including edge_cases.rs, performance.rs, and atomic_safety.rs.

title: Decide handler error surfacing strategy (exit 1 vs exit 0 + JSON error)
description: When handler fails to deliver event to server, how should it surface the error? Two options narrowed down: (A) Exit 1 + stderr message — simple but unclear if user sees it. (B) Exit 0 + JSON response with error field — Claude Code parses it but exit 0 implies success. Need to test actual Claude Code behavior with both approaches. Blocked by ch-20v (test non-JSON hook stdout). Only options A and B are acceptable.

title: Handle spawn race condition: retry POST after failed server spawn
description: When two handlers fire simultaneously during cold start, both detect server is down and try to spawn. One succeeds, one fails. The failing handler should retry the POST (server should be up from the other spawn). v0: retry approach. v1: file lock for proper coordination.

title: Validate hook stdout output against Claude Code SDK schema
description: hookwatch should validate hook stdout (output to Claude Code) in addition to stdin (input from Claude Code). If the JSON output doesn't conform to the expected format (continue, systemMessage, hookSpecificOutput, etc.), log a warning in the web UI. This catches silent failures where Claude Code ignores malformed hook output. Especially important for hookwatch wrap where wrapped commands may leak stdout and corrupt the JSON. Zod schemas for output derived from Claude Code SDK alongside input schemas.

title: Test: does SessionStart hook fire in non-interactive mode?
description: Verify whether the SessionStart hook fires when Claude Code runs in non-interactive mode (e.g., piped input, --print flag, SDK usage). If not, hookwatch may miss session starts in automated/scripted contexts. Test during implementation.

title: Test Claude Code behavior with non-JSON hook stdout
description: Verify what happens when a hook writes non-JSON content to stdout before/after JSON output. Does Claude Code: (a) fail to parse, (b) find the JSON, or (c) silently ignore? This determines whether hookwatch handler needs strict stdout discipline (no console.log, redirect subprocess stdout). Test during implementation.

title: Inject hookwatch context via hook stdout messages
description: Use hook stdout to inject system messages into Claude Code conversations. Examples: SessionStart shows hookwatch status + previous session summary, Stop shows session stats. Each message must identify its source hook AND event type (e.g. '[hookwatch:PreToolUse:Bash] ...'). Explore all hook event types for useful context injection. Constraint: messages must be concise and not block normal user experience. Test which fields are most useful. v0 or v1 depending on complexity.

title: Document data sensitivity risk and set file permissions
description: v0: hookwatch stores all hook stdin payloads in plaintext SQLite, including potential API keys, credentials, and secrets from tool_input. Actions: (1) README section warning users about plaintext storage, (2) create database file with 0600 permissions (owner-only), (3) suggest full-disk encryption for sensitive environments. No runtime encryption in v0 — bun:sqlite doesn't support SQLCipher without custom build.

title: Rename 'daemon' to 'Bun server' in all docs
description: The persistent background process is a Bun server, not a Unix daemon. Update design doc, product brief, PRD, and README. No fork/setsid/PID file — just a long-running Bun process on localhost. Cross-platform compatible.

title: hookwatch open command (v0)
description: CLI command to manually start daemon + open browser. Entry point for browsing old data without waiting for a hook to fire. Identified during Party Mode target users discussion.

title: Implement /v1/messages header fallback for API usage when 403
description:
When /api/oauth/usage returns 403 (scope mismatch — user:inference token lacks user:profile), fall back to sending a 1-token inference request to /v1/messages and parsing rate limit headers:

- anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-utilization → five_hour.utilization
- anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-reset → five_hour.resets_at (Unix → DateTime)
- anthropic-ratelimit-unified-7d-utilization → seven_day.utilization
- anthropic-ratelimit-unified-7d-reset → seven_day.resets_at (Unix → DateTime)

Implementation in crates/claude-usage/src/client.rs:
1. Try /api/oauth/usage first (existing code)
2. On 403 (ApiError::Forbidden), call new fetch_usage_from_headers()
3. fetch_usage_from_headers() sends POST /v1/messages with model=claude-haiku-4-5, max_tokens=1, messages=[{role:user,content:quota}]
4. Parse response headers into UsageData
5. Return UsageData

Uses user:inference scope only. Same approach Claude Code uses internally (kW6() in cli.js). Cost: ~1 token per request, negligible.

See docs/agents/hook-research/oauth-403-research.md for full research.

title: Fix docs: hook_duration_ms tracks OUR handler time, not Claude Code tool duration
description: README, design doc, and product brief incorrectly imply we can measure how long Claude Code's tools take. We only measure how long our handler.ts takes to execute. hook_duration_ms = time from handler start to handler exit. Update all references to be precise about what we measure.

title: Update design doc: Preact + htm replaces Svelte for web UI
description: Svelte requires a build step (conflicts with NFR-5). Preact + htm works without a build pipeline using tagged template literals (~3KB). Update FR-4 and Key Decisions in design doc.

title: Benchmark Bun cold start: handler.ts without daemon
description: Before committing to daemon architecture, benchmark bun run handler.ts cold start (process spawn + SQLite open + Zod load + write + exit). If <100ms is achievable without a daemon, simpler architecture is possible. Run early in implementation.

title: Update design doc: three interfaces (web UI, SQLite, skills)
description: Design doc currently only describes SQLite and web UI. Add the third interface: Claude Code skills shipped with the plugin (v1) for agent-queryable access to hookwatch data. Also add CLI wrap as part of the backend.

title: Ship Claude Code skills as agent interface to hookwatch data
description: Instead of (or in addition to) a CLI, ship skills with the plugin that let the agent query hookwatch's SQLite database. Skills are more natural for agents than raw SQL. Examples: 'show recent events', 'filter by session', 'hook performance summary'. v1 feature.

title: hookwatch wrap CLI: tee-like command wrapper for hook debugging
description: A CLI command (hookwatch wrap <command>) that wraps any hook/bash command, duplicating stdin/stdout/stderr to the hookwatch database. Like tee but for all file descriptors. Users can debug their own hooks by seeing exact I/O in the web UI. v1 feature.

title: API usage shows '--' with no explanation of what went wrong
description:
## Problem

API usage shows '--' (two dashes) in the TUI. The underlying cause is a 403 Forbidden error from the Anthropic API.

## Root Cause

Anthropic blocked subscription OAuth tokens from third-party tools in January 2026. Our claude-usage crate reads the OAuth token from macOS Keychain (stored by Claude Code) and calls `api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage` directly. This is now rejected:

> 'This credential is only authorized for use with Claude Code and cannot be used for other API requests.'

The Feb 19 policy update confirmed: Free, Pro, and Max OAuth tokens cannot be used in third-party tools.

Sources:
- https://openclaw.rocks/blog/anthropic-oauth-ban
- https://winbuzzer.com/2026/02/19/anthropic-bans-claude-subscription-oauth-in-third-party-apps-xcxwbn/
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/13770

## Impact

The entire API usage feature is non-functional. Not fixable by changing headers or endpoints — the token itself is rejected.

## Options

1. Find alternative data source (hooks, transcript, local files)
2. Accept limitation — remove feature or show 'unavailable'
3. Use separate API keys (requires user to have one, different from subscription)

## Immediate fix needed

At minimum, handle 403 gracefully instead of showing '--'. Show a meaningful message like 'API blocked (403)' or 'usage unavailable'.
close_reason: Added Forbidden variant to ApiError, UsageState::Blocked in daemon, 'Quota: blocked' display in TUI, stops retrying after 403. Commit: 36e7a2d

title: Replace PreToolUse(working) with PostToolUse(working) in hook definitions
description: Replace the unfiltered PreToolUse→working hook with PostToolUse→working. PreToolUse fires BEFORE the permission check, so it cannot bridge the attention→working gap after user answers a permission prompt. PostToolUse fires AFTER tool completion, correctly bridging the gap. Also fix the incorrect comment in install.rs (lines 29-31) that says PreToolUse bridges the gap — should say PostToolUse. Also update build.rs plugin generation. Bump project version after change. Source: official docs confirm PreToolUse fires before permission check (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks). Resolves acd-42an. Concludes experiment acd-ws6.
close_reason: Replaced PreToolUse(working) with PostToolUse(working), fixed comment, updated build.rs, bumped to v0.1.4. Merge commit: 61ddf26b740776c9c4bf5263bc09be81aa3d990d

title: TUI stops receiving daemon updates after prolonged inactivity (AFK)
description:
## Problem

After the user is AFK for a long time, the TUI appears to stop receiving updates from the daemon. Sessions may not reflect their current status until the user interacts with the TUI again.

## Suspected causes

- The Unix socket connection between TUI and daemon may time out or be closed after prolonged idle
- The daemon's broadcast subscription may be dropped or stalled
- The TUI's event loop may stop polling the subscription channel during long idle periods
- OS-level socket cleanup (macOS `$TMPDIR` is ephemeral) could invalidate the connection

## Reproduction

1. Start daemon and TUI (`acd daemon start -d && acd tui`)
2. Leave TUI running, go AFK for extended period (exact threshold unknown)
3. Trigger session updates from Claude Code hooks while AFK
4. Return to TUI — session statuses are stale, not reflecting recent updates

## Expected behavior

TUI should maintain its daemon subscription and display live updates regardless of how long the user is away.

## Investigation needed

- Check if the subscription tokio channel is still alive after long idle
- Check if the daemon auto-stop (60 min idle) is killing the daemon while TUI is connected
- Check if the TUI reconnection logic handles a dropped connection gracefully
- Add logging to identify where the connection breaks
notes:
## Code Analysis Findings (2026-02-25)

### Root Cause Identified

TUI has NO reconnection logic after subscription failure.

### Chain of failure:

1. `tui/app/mod.rs:832-838`: subscription spawned once as a fire-and-forget task
2. `tui/subscription.rs:91-104`: when connection breaks (bytes == 0), loop exits silently, returns Ok(())
3. `tui/app/mod.rs:842`: main event loop keeps running but `update_rx.try_recv()` returns empty forever (sender dropped)

### Trigger: daemon auto-stop

- `daemon/mod.rs:29`: AUTO_STOP_IDLE_SECS = 3600
- `daemon/store/mod.rs:227`: has_active_sessions() only counts non-closed, non-inactive sessions — TUI connections don't count
- INACTIVE_SESSION_THRESHOLD = 3600s (lib.rs:49)
- Timeline: sessions go inactive after 1h with no hook events → daemon idle timer starts → daemon auto-stops after idle_timeout (default 1h) → TUI socket breaks silently

### Fix needed

Reconnection loop around subscribe_to_daemon in tui/app/mod.rs, or at minimum a disconnected indicator shown to the user. The subscription task should retry with backoff when the connection drops.

title: Notify user when hook execution fails (exit code, stderr, timeout)
description:
When a TUI hook (activate_hook, reopen_hook) fails, the user currently gets no feedback. Hooks run via `sh -c` in the background and failures are silently swallowed.

Requirements:
1. When a hook exits with non-zero status, show a status bar message in the TUI (e.g., "Hook failed: activate_hook (exit 1)")
2. Write a detailed report to a file (e.g., `~/.local/state/acd/hook-failures.log`) containing:
   - Timestamp
   - Hook type (activate_hook / reopen_hook)
   - Command executed
   - Environment variables passed (ACD_SESSION_ID, ACD_WORKING_DIR, ACD_STATUS)
   - Exit code
   - Captured stderr (and stdout if any)
   - Duration
3. For timeouts, report "Hook timed out after Xs" instead of exit code
4. Status bar message should mention the log file path so user knows where to look

Depends on acd-ynba (env vars) and relates to acd-hgaz (hook execution redesign with timeouts and output capture).

title: Implement hot-reload for TUI config (hooks, tick_rate, layout)
description:
TUI config values (activate_hook, reopen_hook, tick_rate, layout, widgets) are read once at startup and cached in App fields. Changing the config file has no effect until full restart (daemon + TUI).

The default config template claims 'Hot-reloadable: Yes' for hooks — this is incorrect. Remove that claim or implement actual hot-reload.

Implementation options:
1. File watcher (notify crate) on config.toml, reload on change
2. Periodic re-read (e.g., every 30s)
3. Manual reload via keypress (e.g., 'R' to reload config)

Option 1 is the standard approach. The TUI event loop already polls for events — add a config watcher channel.

Fields to hot-reload:
- activate_hook, reopen_hook (read from config on each use, not cached)
- tick_rate (requires recreating the tick interval)
- layout, widgets (requires re-rendering)

Simplest first step: re-read hooks from config on each execute_hook() call instead of caching. This avoids the file watcher complexity and covers the most common case (user edits hook, tries again).

Also fix the default config template: remove 'Hot-reloadable: Yes' claims until actually implemented.

title: Redesign TUI hooks: array format with timeout, output capture, and env vars
description:
Current TUI hooks (activate_hook, reopen_hook) are single strings executed via sh -c. This is limited and unsafe. Redesign to match Claude Code's hook structure before v1.0.

## Current (broken)

```toml
activate_hook = "zellij action go-to-tab-name $(basename {working_dir})"
```

Problems:
1. Single hook per event (can't run multiple commands)
2. No timeout (hung hook freezes TUI)
3. No output capture (stdout/stderr lost)
4. String substitution for placeholders (shell injection risk, acd-ynba)
5. TOML escaping is painful for shell commands with quotes

## Target design

Array of hook objects, matching Claude Code's structure:

```toml
[[tui.activate_hooks]]
command = 'zellij action go-to-tab-name "$(basename "$ACD_WORKING_DIR")" --session "$ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME"'
timeout = 5

[[tui.activate_hooks]]
command = 'echo "activated" >> /tmp/acd-hooks.log'
timeout = 2

[[tui.reopen_hooks]]
command = 'zellij action new-tab --name "$(basename "$ACD_WORKING_DIR")" --cwd "$ACD_WORKING_DIR" --session "$ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME"'
timeout = 5
```

## Claude Code hook format (reference)

Claude Code hooks use this JSON structure in settings.json:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "EventName": [
      {
        "matcher": "optional_pattern",
        "hooks": [
          {
            "type": "command",
            "command": "my-command \"arg\"",
            "timeout": 10
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Key properties:
- Array of hook groups per event, each group has optional matcher
- Each group contains array of hooks (multiple commands per event)
- Each hook has type, command, and timeout (seconds)
- Environment variables like $CLAUDE_HOOK_INPUT_session_id for data passing
- Stdin receives JSON payload

## Changes needed

1. Config schema: activate_hook (String) → activate_hooks (Vec<HookConfig>)
2. HookConfig struct: { command: String, timeout: u64 (seconds, default 5) }
3. Execution: run all hooks in sequence (or parallel?), respect timeout, kill on exceed
4. Output: capture stdout/stderr, log at debug level
5. Env vars: ACD_SESSION_ID, ACD_WORKING_DIR, ACD_STATUS (from acd-ynba)
6. Stdin: still pipe JSON SessionSnapshot
7. Migration: if old string format detected, treat as single hook with default timeout
8. Update default config template with new format and examples

## Blocked by

- acd-ynba (env vars must land first, or do both together)

## Related

- acd-xu1w (security audit)
- acd-ynba (env vars for hooks)
notes:
Design decisions (confirmed by user):
- Sequential execution: hooks run one after another, not parallel
- No backward compatibility: old `activate_hook = "..."` string format just breaks. No migration, no serde alias. User will see the error and update their config
- Field rename: `activate_hook` → `activate_hooks`, `reopen_hook` → `reopen_hooks` (plural, array)
- Config template style: provide real TOML entries that are commented out with "uncomment to enable" guidance, not empty strings with examples in comments above
- HookConfig struct: { command: String, timeout: u64 } with default timeout (e.g., 5 seconds)
- Execution happens in the TUI event handler (double-click path), iterating hooks sequentially with spawn + wait/timeout per hook
- Env vars (ACD_SESSION_ID, ACD_WORKING_DIR, ACD_STATUS) already landed in acd-ynba
- JSON stdin still piped to each hook
close_reason: Redesigned TUI hooks to array format with HookConfig struct, sequential execution, per-hook timeout, stdout/stderr capture. Commit: 5159226

title: Security audit: review all string substitution and shell execution paths before v1.0
description:
Before publishing v1.0, audit all code paths where user-provided or external strings are passed to shell execution (sh -c, Command::new, etc.) or used in string substitution/interpolation.

Known instance (being fixed):
- acd-ynba: hook placeholder substitution is a shell injection vector

Audit checklist:
1. All Command::new() and sh -c calls — are arguments properly escaped or passed as env vars?
2. All string .replace() / format!() that produce shell commands — any injection risk?
3. Config values used in shell contexts — can a malicious config value escape?
4. IPC command fields — can a malicious IPC client inject via session_id, working_dir, etc.?
5. Any other place where external input flows into shell execution

This is a blocking issue for v1.0 release.

title: Replace hook placeholder substitution with environment variables
description:
Current hook system uses string substitution for {session_id}, {working_dir}, {status} placeholders in hook commands before passing to sh -c. This is:

1. **Dangerous** — shell injection if working_dir contains metacharacters (spaces, quotes, backticks)
2. **Non-standard** — {placeholder} format is not a recognized convention
3. **Confusing** — users don't know what expands them

Fix: Set environment variables on the child process instead of string substitution.

```rust
Command::new("sh")
    .arg("-c")
    .arg(&hook_cmd)  // no substitution
    .env("ACD_SESSION_ID", &session.session_id)
    .env("ACD_WORKING_DIR", &working_dir_str)
    .env("ACD_STATUS", &session.status.to_string())
    .stdin(Stdio::piped())
```

Config examples become:
```toml
activate_hook = "zellij action go-to-tab-name \"\$(basename \"\$ACD_WORKING_DIR\")\""
```

Changes needed:
1. Remove `substitute_hook_placeholders()` from `tui/app/mod.rs`
2. Add .env() calls in `execute_hook()`
3. Update default config examples in `config/default.rs` to use $ACD_* env vars
4. Update tests that reference old placeholder format
5. JSON stdin still piped for advanced hooks (unchanged)

Prefix with ACD_ to avoid collisions.
notes:
Decisions from user:
- No backward compatibility — delete substitute_hook_placeholders() cleanly, old {placeholder} format stops working
- TOML escaping: don't try to verify hook commands work. If user's shell syntax is wrong, hook fails and they see it (acd-xxu9 will add failure notifications later)
- Add escaping guidance in config comments — mention that TOML basic strings need backslash escaping, and suggest triple-quoted TOML strings (like Python multi-line strings) for complex commands
- Agent runs cargo test; any tests referencing old placeholder format will fail and need updating
- No issue splitting — single logical change, one agent (hook-ipc)
close_reason: Removed substitute_hook_placeholders(), added ACD_SESSION_ID/ACD_WORKING_DIR/ACD_STATUS env vars to hook execution, updated config examples and docs. 714 tests (5 placeholder tests removed). Commit: 3339c84ddb1d36ffb3234168122bc9fb95bdbad8

title: config edit fails when EDITOR contains arguments
description:
Command::new(&editor) in commands/daemon.rs:214 passes the entire EDITOR string as the binary name. When EDITOR='code-insiders --wait', it calls execve with the whole string as the binary path — no word splitting (that's a shell feature, not a kernel feature).

Fix: Run through shell (same approach as git, crontab, visudo):

```rust
Command::new("sh")
    .arg("-c")
    .arg(format!("{} \"\\"", &editor))
    .arg("--")
    .arg(&config_path)
    .status()
```

This lets the shell handle word splitting for EDITOR values like 'code-insiders --wait', 'vim -u NONE', etc.

Affects both VISUAL and EDITOR. Common values that break: 'code --wait', 'code-insiders --wait', 'subl --wait', 'zed --wait'.
close_reason: EDITOR now runs through sh -c for proper word splitting. Commit: 828164264fc463955288f4fe521ec0adc5579dcf

title: Add session_id and status to daemon log lines on SET command
description:
handle_set_command() in handlers/mod.rs has zero logging. When a hook fires and sets a session status, there's no log line showing which session changed to which status. This makes debugging stuck-status bugs (acd-42an, acd-4cqb) impossible from the log alone.

Fix: Add a debug! or info! log line in handle_set_command() that includes session_id (short prefix is fine) and the new status. Example: 'SET session=abc12345 status=working working_dir=/Users/pablo/project'

Also consider logging in get_or_create_session() whether the session was created vs updated.
close_reason: Added info! log in handle_set_command (session_id, status, working_dir) and debug! logs in get_or_create_session (created vs updated). Commit: 0f693df7ebf607ee786b5c8f81273f2a2c7b8568

title: Set default activate_hook and reopen_hook commands
description:
Currently activate_hook and reopen_hook default to empty strings, meaning double-click/Enter does nothing out of the box. Users must manually configure them.

We should provide sensible defaults so the TUI works immediately after install. Needs research into what commands work cross-platform and across terminal multiplexers.

Considerations:
- activate_hook (non-closed session): should focus/switch to the session's terminal. Possible approaches: open a new terminal window at {working_dir}, or integrate with tmux/Zellij to focus the pane.
- reopen_hook (closed session): should resume the Claude Code session. Likely 'claude --resume' or similar.
- Defaults must be safe (no destructive side effects) and fail gracefully if the command doesn't exist.
- May need platform detection (macOS vs Linux) for terminal commands.
- Consider whether the default should be a no-op with a helpful message vs an actual command.
close_reason: Added richer hook examples (Zellij, VS Code, tmux, Terminal) to default config template. Commit: cdd4e33

title: Fetch API usage on daemon start instead of waiting 3 minutes
description:
The usage fetcher deliberately skips the first tick (daemon/usage.rs line 97-99), meaning the first usage data arrives only after the configured interval (default 3m). This causes the TUI to show '--' for up to 3 minutes on startup.

Fix: remove the first-tick skip so fetch_once() runs immediately at daemon start. The fetch_once() method already handles zero subscribers gracefully (skips the fetch), but even that case is fine — once a subscriber connects, the next tick will fetch.

Alternatively, trigger an immediate fetch when the first subscriber connects (handler sends subscribe command).

Files: crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/daemon/usage.rs (run method, lines 95-99)
close_reason: Removed first-tick skip in usage fetcher. Commit: ea1ec48

title: Add easier daemon log access (foreground mode or log file)
description:
Currently, debugging the daemon requires AGENT_CONSOLE_DASHBOARD_LOG=debug acd daemon start, but:

1. Logs go to stderr which is lost when daemon runs in background
2. If a daemon is already running, the new one silently skips — user gets 'reusing existing daemon' with no debug output
3. No way to view logs after the fact

Options to consider:
- acd daemon start --foreground: run daemon in foreground (no detach), logs visible in terminal
- acd daemon logs: tail the daemon log file (requires daemon to write to a log file)
- acd daemon start --log-file <path>: redirect logs to a file
- Combination: always write to a log file, add 'acd daemon logs' to tail it

The foreground mode is the simplest to implement and most useful for debugging. Log file approach is better for post-mortem investigation.
notes:
Design decisions:
- Default log_file: XDG data path (~/.local/share/agent-console-dashboard/daemon.log)
- No --foreground flag (not in scope)
- Wire up existing log_file config field to logging::init()
- When log_file is empty string, use XDG data path as default (not stderr)
- logging::init() needs to accept optional path parameter
- Ensure parent directory is created if it doesn't exist

Implementation:
1. Update logging::init() to accept Option<PathBuf> for log file
2. When Some(path), write to file; when None, write to stderr
3. In run_daemon(), read config log_file field, default to XDG data path if empty
4. Update default config template: log_file default should be empty (meaning XDG default)
5. Add acd daemon logs command to tail the log file (nice to have)
close_reason: Wired up log_file config, default XDG data path. Commit: 2a9b11b

title: Dynamic chip width (max 18) and truncate from start
description:
Two changes to two-line layout chips:

1. **Truncate from the start**: keep the end of long folder names. E.g., 'my-long-folder-name' → '...folder-name' (not 'my-long-fo...').

2. **Dynamic chip width**: chips should be as wide as their content, up to a max of 18. Short folder names (e.g., 'src') should not be padded to 18 chars. This means CHIP_WIDTH becomes MAX_CHIP_WIDTH, and actual chip widths vary.

Impact: pagination math (calculate_max_visible_chips, mouse click coordinate mapping in calculate_clicked_chip) currently assumes uniform CHIP_WIDTH. With dynamic widths, these need to compute based on actual rendered chip widths — either by storing per-chip widths or recalculating from session data.

Files: crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/tui/ui.rs (render_compact_session_chips, calculate_max_visible_chips), crates/agent-console-dashboard/src/tui/app/mod.rs (calculate_clicked_chip, calculate_max_visible_chips_for_mouse), related tests.
notes:
Design decision: pipe-separated chips with brackets only on focused item.

Layout examples:
  With overflow:  <- 3+| * agent-console | ! my-project |[? src]| . old-proj |5+ ->
  No overflow:    <- 0 | * agent-console | ! my-project |[? src]| . old-proj | 0 ->

Overflow indicators:
- ALWAYS shown, never hidden, never grayed out
- Count > 0: N+ with no space before pipe: '<- 3+|' and '|5+ ->'
- Count = 0: 0 with space (no plus): '<- 0 |' and '| 0 ->'
- This keeps width consistent: '+' replaces the space when count > 0
- Pipes at both ends always present regardless of count

Chip anatomy:
- Unselected: ' ' + symbol + ' ' + name (e.g., ' * my-proj')
- Focused: '[' + symbol + ' ' + name + ']' (e.g., '[? src]')
- Separator: ' | ' between chips, but NO extra space adjacent to brackets
  - unfocused | unfocused: ' | '
  - unfocused |[focused: ' |['
  - focused]| unfocused: ']| '
- Pipe separators between overflow indicators and first/last chip included above

Truncation: from start, keep end. E.g., '...folder-name'.
Dynamic width: no padding — chip is as wide as its content, max 18.
No minimum chip width — size to content.
close_reason: Dynamic chip width, pipe separators, start truncation. Commit: a2628c9

title: Status stays attention after permission prompt is answered
description:
After a permission_prompt notification fires (status → attention), the user answers the permission. The status should revert to 'working' because Claude resumes execution. However, it stays on 'attention'.

Hypothesis: PreToolUse was added (line 29-32 of install.rs) specifically to bridge this gap, but it may not fire for the tool that was paused for permission — only for subsequent tools. If the approved tool executes without a new PreToolUse event, status stays 'attention' until the next tool use.

Related: acd-4cqb (similar stuck-status issue but for cancel/ESC). Both may share a root cause — hook events not firing when expected.

Investigation needed:
1. Manual test with debug logging: answer permission prompt, check if PreToolUse fires
2. If PreToolUse does NOT fire after permission approval, check if PostToolUse fires (currently disabled in experiment acd-ws6)
3. If neither fires, consider restoring PostToolUse or adding a new hook event

Agent: hook-research (investigation) then hook-ipc (fix if possible).
notes: Design review (2026-02-26): Root cause confirmed — PreToolUse fires BEFORE permission check, not after. Fix: replace PreToolUse(working) with PostToolUse(working). Implementation tracked in acd-0amx.

title: Status stuck on working after user cancels with ESC/Ctrl+C
description:
When a user sends a prompt then interrupts it (ESC or Ctrl+C), the session status remains "working" instead of reverting to "attention".

Expected: after cancel/interrupt, status should revert to "attention" (the idle state for an active session).
Actual: status stays locked on "working".

Root cause hypothesis: Claude Code may not fire a Stop hook event on user interruption/cancellation. If so, this may be a Claude Code behavior we cannot control — need to investigate which hook events fire on ESC/Ctrl+C.

Investigation steps:
1. Check which hook events Claude Code fires on ESC/Ctrl+C (UserPromptSubmit fires on send, but does Stop fire on cancel?)
2. If Stop does not fire on cancel, check if any other event fires that we could use
3. If no event fires, this may need to be reported as a Claude Code feature request

Agent: hook-research (investigation) then hook-ipc (fix if possible).
notes: DESIGN REVIEW (2026-02-25): Confirmed design gap — not a code bug. Official Claude Code docs (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks, Stop section) state: 'Does not run if the stoppage occurred due to a user interrupt.' When user presses ESC/Ctrl+C, NO hook event fires (Stop explicitly excluded). PostToolUseFailure with is_interrupt only fires if a tool was mid-execution, not during thinking/streaming. This is a Claude Code limitation, not an ACD bug. DECISION: Accept for now. Next step: file feature request — tracked in acd-do85. Timeout fallback rejected — sessions can legitimately work 1h+ without status change, would false-trigger.

title: CLI: acd session delete command
description:
Add CLI command: acd session delete <session_id>

- Sends DELETE IPC message to daemon (command string: "DELETE")
- On success: print the returned SessionSnapshot (option b)

description:
Add a daemon IPC handler to delete a session from the store.

- Handler removes the session from SessionStore entirely (not just set status to closed)

title: TUI stops receiving daemon updates after idle period
description: After being AFK for an extended period, the TUI stops displaying new updates from the daemon. Sessions appear frozen/stale. Likely a connection timeout or missed reconnect. Needs investigation into the WebSocket/Unix socket connection lifecycle and keep-alive behavior. Agent: daemon-core or tui-rendering.
close_reason: Research complete. Root cause: no reconnection logic in TUI subscription. Fix: retry loop with backoff. Doc: docs/agents/daemon-core/tui-stale-data-investigation.md. Commit: 89ff5d6

title: CLI: remove/drop session from daemon list
description:
Parent issue. Split into 3 sub-issues:
- acd-mf45: Daemon handler for session delete IPC (daemon-core)
- acd-tmk9: CLI command acd session delete (cli-features), blocked by mf45
- acd-wtr7: Research broadcast complexity (daemon-core)

Command naming follows CRUD: Create/Read/Update/Delete → acd session delete <id>

title: Dim all columns consistently for inactive/closed sessions
description: Currently, inactive and closed session rows have mismatched colors: the status text (e.g., "inactive", "closed") is more visible than other columns (directory, session_id, time elapsed, priority). All columns in inactive/closed rows should use the SAME color as the status column text. This means directory, session_id, time elapsed, and priority columns should all render in the same dimmed color as the status word. Agent: tui-visual.
close_reason: All columns now consistently dimmed for inactive/closed sessions. Commit: 0dfe215

title: Investigate: inactive session reactivation via hooks
description: When a session becomes inactive (dimmed due to INACTIVE_SESSION_THRESHOLD), it is unclear whether a new hook event for that session properly reactivates it (un-dims it). Questions: (1) Does a hook event update the session since timestamp to reactivate it? (2) If /clear creates a new session_id, the old session can never receive new hooks — it stays inactive forever. (3) Should the daemon garbage-collect orphaned inactive sessions for the same directory? Related to session_id stability investigation.
notes: Blocked on manual testing for v0.1.3. Part of epic acd-slxu. Test plan: use acd session delete to clean orphans, copy session IDs with s key, verify --resume behavior.

title: Investigate: does /clear create a new session_id?
description:
User assumption: /clear creates a new session_id (UUID), /resume preserves the existing one. Verify this by searching Claude Code documentation, source code references, or official docs.

**Deliverable:** A short, concrete answer for each:
1. Does /clear generate a new session_id UUID? (expected: yes)
2. Does /compact preserve the session_id UUID?
3. Does /resume preserve the session_id UUID? (expected: yes)

**Must include references** — link to docs, source code, or official documentation that confirms each answer. If no authoritative source exists, state that explicitly.

If /clear creates new IDs, note implications: old session stays as orphan in daemon, same directory gets multiple session entries.

Agent: hook-research (research mode). Output: research report in docs/agents/hook-research/.
close_reason: session_id NOT stable. /clear, /compact, /resume all generate new UUIDs. Confirmed via GitHub issue #12235. Report: docs/agents/hook-research/session-id-lifecycle.md. Commit: 89e305c71fdbc2eac6750dc2205d54a30e71fb83

title: History display: per-state duration format in detail panel
description:
Change the detail panel history display from 'time since transition' to 'duration in each state'.

Current: shows relative time like 'some time ago' for each transition.
New: shows per-state duration in the most recent state transition sequence.
Example: 'working 10m | attention 2m | question 30s'

- Show the most recent transition sequence (not aggregated totals across all visits to a state)
- All entries except the last are FIXED (historical, won't change)
- The LAST entry is DYNAMIC: shows time from last transition to now, updates once per second (same tick rate as current implementation)
- Duration format: use the CURRENT time format function as-is (no format changes in this issue)
- Overflow handling: NOT in scope (separate TUI issue)
- Time format refactoring: NOT in scope (separate refactor issue)

Scope is strictly: change the data source from 'time since transition' to 'duration in each state'. No TUI layout changes, no format changes.

title: TUI: show API usage in bottom-right of full-width layout footer
description:
Show API usage SHORT mode in bottom-right of large layout footer. Split footer: keybinding hints left, API usage right.

SHORT format: [5h:8% 7d:77%] (brackets style, no spaces around colon)

Format: 'v0.1.2' right-aligned in the header block. Remove version from footer — bottom-right reserved for API usage (acd-0i4i). Files: src/tui/ui.rs, update footer/header tests.

title: TUI: add priority column and reorder columns to match sort order
description:
## Current state

Full TUI columns: Directory (flex) | Session ID (40) | Status (14) | Time Elapsed (16). No priority column exists.

## Required changes

1. Add a Priority column (12 chars wide) showing the session priority value (u64, default 0)
2. Reorder columns to match sort precedence: Directory (flex) | Status (14) | Priority (12) | Time Elapsed (16) | Session ID (40)

Column order rationale: sort-key columns (Status, Priority, Time Elapsed) appear in sort precedence order. Directory is first as the primary visual identifier. Session ID is last.

Sort order for reference: status group → priority (desc) → time elapsed.

## Column widths

| Column | Width | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Directory | flex | Basename with parent disambiguation |
| Status | 14 | Fixed |
| Priority | 12 | New column. Header: "Priority" (8 chars + padding) |
| Time Elapsed | 16 | Fixed. Header: "Time Elapsed" |
| Session ID | 40 | UUID v4 (36 chars + padding) |

## Files to modify

- `src/tui/views/dashboard/mod.rs` — `format_session_line()`, `format_header_line()`
- `docs/design/ui.md` — update column width documentation

## Context

- Priority field already exists in SessionSnapshot (u64, default 0)
- Column labels are LEFT-aligned
- Cell content is LEFT-aligned, all padding trailing
- Highlight marker: filled triangle, HighlightSpacing::Always
close_reason: Priority column added (12 chars), columns reordered to match sort precedence: Dir → Status → Priority → Time Elapsed → Session ID. Commit: ca3ff95

title: TUI regression: directory column shows full path instead of basename
description:
## Observed behavior

Directory column in the full TUI shows the complete path (e.g.,
`/home/user/projects/my-app`) instead of just the basename (`my-app`).

## Expected behavior

Directory column shows basename by default. When multiple sessions share
the same basename, parent folders are added for disambiguation (up to 3
levels). This logic exists in `compute_directory_display_names()` at
`tui/views/dashboard/mod.rs:75`.

## Investigation notes

The code uses `path.file_name()` which should extract the basename. The
regression may be in:
- How `working_dir` is sent from hooks to daemon (full path vs normalized)
- An edge case in path handling (trailing slash, symlinks, etc.)
- Data-level issue rather than display-level issue

## Design question (deferred)

Disambiguation currently lives only in the TUI. Should the daemon also
compute display names? Separate issue for this design discussion.

## Reproduction

Run `acd tui` with active Claude Code sessions. Observe the Directory column.
close_reason: Not a bug. User had duplicate folder names with different session IDs, triggering disambiguation (expected behavior).

title: CLI tree redesign: group subcommands by domain
description:
## Problem

Current CLI tree is flat and disorganized. Too many top-level commands (width)
without enough depth. Commands from different domains (daemon, session, config)
are mixed at the same level.

## Current tree (v0.1.2)

```text
acd
├── tui [--socket]
├── status [--socket]                          # daemon-specific
├── dump [--socket] [--format]                 # daemon-specific
├── config {init,path,validate,show,edit}
├── resurrect <session_id> [--socket] [--quiet]  # session-specific
├── set <session_id> <status> [--working-dir]    # session-specific
├── daemon {start,stop,restart}
├── claude-hook <status> [--socket]            # internal hook handler
├── install
└── uninstall
```

## Proposed tree

```text
acd
├── tui [--socket]
├── daemon
│   ├── start [--socket] [--detach]
│   ├── stop [--socket] [--force]
│   ├── restart [--socket] [--detach]
│   ├── status [--socket]                      ← moved from top level
│   └── dump [--socket] [--format]             ← moved from top level
├── session
│   ├── set <id> <status> [--priority] [--working-dir]  ← moved, extended
│   ├── list [--socket]                        ← new
│   └── reopen <id> [--socket]                 ← renamed from resurrect, moved
├── config
│   ├── init [--force]
│   ├── path
│   ├── validate
│   ├── show
│   └── edit
├── claude-hook <status> [--socket]            # stays (internal, referenced by hooks)
├── install
└── uninstall
```

## Key decisions (from discussion)

- `status` and `dump` move under `daemon` — they are daemon diagnostics
- `set` and `resurrect` move under `session` — they are session operations
- `resurrect` renamed to `reopen` (see acd-bwa for hook design)
- `--priority` added to `session set` (Option A from priority discussion)
- `claude-hook` stays at top level (internal, referenced by installed hooks)
- Clean break (no backwards-compat aliases) — pre-1.0, no external users
- `session list` added — currently sessions only visible via dump or TUI
- `tui`, `config`, `install`, `uninstall` stay at top level

## Scope

- Move existing commands into new groups (clap nested subcommands)
- Update `acd install` to reference new paths if any hook commands changed
- Update tests that reference old command names
- Update docs referencing old commands
notes:
Design question resolved (2026-02-15): Use single `update` command.

`acd session update <id> [--status] [--priority] [--working-dir]`

Reasons: atomic (one IPC call), conventional (matches kubectl/gh/docker),
fewer keystrokes. `set` rejected as primary name — `update` is the standard
CRUD verb for partial modification. No flags = warning, nonexistent ID = error.

Decision document: docs/decisions/session-update-command.md

Scope narrowed (2026-02-15):
- `session reopen` removed — reopen is TUI-only (acd-bwa). Deferred: acd-9ws (P4)
- `session list` removed — not needed for v0. Deferred: acd-eem (P4)
- `session activate` removed — not needed for v0. Deferred: acd-4of (P4)
- Remaining scope: restructure CLI tree (move commands into groups) + rename set → update
close_reason: CLI tree restructured: status/dump under daemon, set→update under session. 5 files, 7 new tests. Commit: 0808a65

title: TUI selection highlight off by 1 in normal mode, off by 2 in debug mode
description:
When clicking a session row in the TUI, the selection highlight lands on the wrong row. In normal mode it's off by 1 (selects the row below the clicked one). In debug mode (AGENT_CONSOLE_DASHBOARD_DEBUG=1) it's off by 2.

## Root Cause

calculate_clicked_session() in src/tui/app/mod.rs:231 uses a hardcoded offset of 2 (app header + column header). It doesn't account for:
1. The Block top border on the List widget (+1 row) — causes off-by-1 in normal mode
2. The debug ruler row (+1 more) — causes off-by-2 in debug mode

Actual row positions:
- Normal mode: App header (0) → Column header (1) → Block border (2) → Session 0 (3) — offset should be 3
- Debug mode: + debug ruler row — offset should be 4
- Narrow mode (width < 40): No column header, no ruler — offset 2 is correct

## Design Decision: Store list_area Rect from render pass (Option C)

Do NOT fix by changing the hardcoded offset. Instead:

1. Add a field to App: session_list_inner_area: Rect (the List widget's inner area, excluding block borders)
2. During render_session_list(), after computing list_area and the Block, calculate the inner Rect (list_area with border insets) and store it on App via a mutable reference or return value
3. In calculate_clicked_session(), use the stored Rect: check mouse.row falls within session_list_inner_area, then compute index as (mouse.row - session_list_inner_area.y) as usize
4. This automatically handles normal mode, debug mode, narrow mode, and any future layout changes

Key files:
- src/tui/app/mod.rs — calculate_clicked_session() and App struct
- src/tui/views/dashboard/mod.rs — render_session_list() where list_area is computed
- src/tui/ui.rs — render_dashboard() orchestrates the render
- src/tui/app/tests/interaction.rs — update existing tests for new offset behavior

The render pass already knows the exact geometry. Storing it eliminates all hardcoded offsets and prevents future regressions when layout changes.
close_reason: Fixed: stored inner Rect from render pass, rewrote tests as pure unit tests

title: CLI command to set session priority
description:
Add `--priority <u64>` flag to the SET command (Option A — extend existing command).

Priority is a u64 unsigned integer. Default: 0. Higher value = ranked higher in
the TUI session list (see acd-wx6 for sort order).

## Implementation

1. Add `priority: u64` field to `Session` struct (default 0)
2. Add `priority: u64` field to `SessionSnapshot` (`#[serde(default)]`)
3. Include priority in `From<&Session> for SessionSnapshot`
4. Add `priority: Option<u64>` to `IpcCommand`
5. Extend `handle_set_command` to update priority when provided
6. Rename `broadcast_status_change` → `broadcast_session_change`; fire on
   status OR priority change
7. Add `--priority <u64>` to CLI `set` subcommand
8. Tests for priority storage, broadcast, and IPC round-trip

## CLI (current tree, pre-redesign)

```text
acd set <session_id> <status> --priority <u64> [--working-dir <path>]
```

After CLI tree redesign (acd-2jp), this becomes `acd session set ...`.

## IPC

```json
{"version": 1, "cmd": "SET", "session_id": "abc", "status": "working", "priority": 5}
```

Priority-only updates (no status change) are deferred — requires making status
optional in SET, which is part of acd-2jp scope.
notes:
Design decisions (session):
- Option A chosen: extend SET command with --priority flag
- No separate PRIORITY IPC command
- Priority-only updates (without status) deferred to CLI tree redesign (acd-2jp)
  where status becomes optional in session set
- broadcast_status_change → broadcast_session_change (fires on any change)
close_reason: Implemented: session sorting + CLI priority flag

title: Config init --force: show backup path and new file path in console
description:
When running 'acd config init --force' and an existing config is found:
1. No feedback is shown during backup
2. User doesn't know where the old config was backed up to

Expected output:
  Old config backed up to: /path/to/config.toml.bak.20260213T143052Z
  Created new config: /path/to/config.toml

If --force is used but no existing config exists, behave as if --force was not passed (just create a new config normally).

Backup filename format: <name>.bak.<tinydate> where tinydate uses Zulu time: YYYYMMDDTHHmmssZ (16 chars). Example: config.toml.bak.20260213T143052Z

Note: 'acd config init' (without --force) already prints the path — no change needed there.

title: Document focus interaction model
description:
Document the focus interaction model in docs/design/ui.md.

The detail panel always reflects the currently focused session. Focus is the central concept — every interaction that changes focus also updates the detail panel.

Actions that change focus:
- Scroll wheel (mouse wheel) → changes focus to the scrolled-to item
- Left click on unfocused item → changes focus to the clicked item
- Double click on any item → changes focus + triggers hook action
- j/k keyboard navigation → changes focus to the next/previous item

Actions that do NOT change focus:
- Left click on the already-focused item → no-op (focus unchanged)

Special behaviors:
- Double click on the already-focused item → still triggers hook action (focus stays, hook fires)
- Enter on focused item → same as double-click (focus + trigger hook)
- Esc / click header → defocus (hide detail panel, no item focused)

Current gap: scroll wheel changes selection but doesn't show detail panel. This should be fixed (related: acd-bbm).
close_reason: Documented focus interaction model in docs/design/ui.md

title: Investigate: session resume/continue doesn't fire SessionStart hook
description:
SessionStart fires on resume (source field = 'resume'|'clear'|'compact'|'startup'). But user observed that resuming a closed session didn't update TUI.

Possible explanations:
1. SessionStart fired but daemon treated it as update to existing closed session (no status change visible)
2. The daemon receives 'attention' for SessionStart — if session was already 'closed', does it reopen?
3. Race condition between hook and TUI refresh

Current daemon behavior: when a SET command arrives for a session that was marked closed, does it reopen? Need to verify in store code.

Key: session_id is stable across resume/clear/compact. The daemon will see the same session_id for the resumed session.
close_reason: Non-issue: user already has direct visibility when resuming a session. Next UserPromptSubmit hook updates TUI within seconds anyway.

title: Pass SessionSnapshot as stdin JSON to double-click hook
description:
When the double-click hook fires, pipe the full SessionSnapshot as JSON to the hook command's stdin. This follows the same pattern as Claude Code hooks (JSON on stdin).

Implementation:
1. Serialize the SessionSnapshot for the selected session to JSON
2. Pipe it to the hook command's stdin (change Stdio::null() to Stdio::piped(), write JSON)
3. Document the JSON structure in docs/user/ (create file if needed)
4. Ensure SessionSnapshot is re-exported from the library crate (idempotent — check if already done, add only if missing)

The hook receives everything: session_id, working_dir, status, status history, timestamps, etc.

Code location (after file split refactoring):
- Function: execute_double_click_hook() at src/tui/app/mod.rs:174
- Currently uses std::process::Command with sh -c, fire-and-forget
- stdout/stderr piped to Stdio::null(), stdin is implicit (not set)
- The change: set .stdin(Stdio::piped()), write SessionSnapshot JSON to child stdin
- SessionSnapshot is defined in src/ipc.rs (re-exported from lib.rs via pub use ipc::*)
- Tests for double-click behavior: src/tui/app/tests/interaction.rs
close_reason: SessionSnapshot piped as JSON to double-click hook stdin. Tests added, docs created.

This causes infinite recursion because the global hook already chains back to the local hook. Fix: remove lines 11-19 (the GLOBAL_HOOKS_DIR block) from scripts/pre-commit.sh. The global hook runs first via core.hooksPath, then chains to local. Local should NOT call global back.

title: E2E integration test with real Claude Code
description:
## Problem

No automated or documented end-to-end test verifying the full ACD flow with a real
Claude Code instance. A manual smoke test passed (2026-02-08) but the procedure is
not documented and not repeatable by others.

## What Was Tested Manually

1. cargo install → acd in PATH
2. acd install → hooks written to ~/.claude/settings.json
3. Start Claude Code session → hooks fire
4. Daemon lazy-starts from hook
5. Sessions tracked in daemon (verified via acd dump)
6. acd tui shows live session
7. acd uninstall → hooks removed

## What Needs Documentation/Automation

### Test Plan Document

Write a reproducible test plan covering:

- Prerequisites (Claude Code installed, cargo, Rust toolchain)
- Step-by-step procedure with expected outputs
- Verification commands at each step
- Known issues (e.g., env__fetch_usage_raw panics without CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN)

### Automated Smoke Test (stretch goal)

A script that automates what can be automated:
- cargo install
- acd install → verify hooks in settings.json
- acd daemon → start daemon
- Simulate hook calls (echo JSON | acd claude-hook attention)
- acd dump → verify session exists
- acd uninstall → verify hooks removed

The Claude Code interaction itself cannot be automated (requires human session).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Documented test plan in docs/testing/ or similar
- At least the non-interactive parts scriptable
- CI could optionally run the automated portion
close_reason: E2E test plan + automated smoke script. All tests pass.

title: Config documentation and acd config show command
description:
## Problem

No user-facing documentation on config options. Users don't know what settings exist,
what the defaults are, or how to customize behavior. Three gaps:

1. **No `acd config show`**: Users can create a config (`acd config init`), check
   the path (`acd config path`), and validate (`acd config validate`), but cannot
   view current effective values (defaults + file overrides + env var overrides).

2. **No config reference docs**: The TOML schema defines fields like
   `socket_path`, `tick_interval_secs`, `usage_fetch_interval`, `auto_stop_idle_secs`,
   etc. but these are only documented in the source code. No user-facing reference.

3. **No `--help` integration**: `acd config --help` shows subcommands but doesn't
   explain what configuration controls.

## Context

Config system implemented in E007. Config loading, XDG paths, TOML parsing all work.
The schema is defined in `src/config/` module. Default values documented in open
questions Q39. Environment variable overrides supported per Q64.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `acd config show` displays current effective config (resolved from defaults + file + env)
- Config reference section added to README or as separate `docs/configuration.md`
- Each config key documented with: type, default, description, env var override name
close_reason: acd config show displays effective config as TOML with source header. Committed 83ba356.

title: claude-usage: OAuth token is cached and never refreshed
description:
The claude-usage crate reads the OAuth token once (from Keychain on macOS, from credential file on Linux) and uses it for all subsequent API calls during the daemon's lifetime. If the token expires or is refreshed by Claude Code (e.g., after re-login), the daemon continues using the stale cached token.

This is separate from acd-o4z1 (header fallback for 403). Even after implementing the header fallback, the token used for /v1/messages inference requests would also be stale if not refreshed.

Needs investigation:
- How does Claude Code refresh its OAuth token? (auto-refresh on expiry?)
- Should the daemon re-read the token on each fetch cycle?
- What is the token TTL? (claude.ai login gives 1-year token per research)

title: hookwatch wrap: Bun.spawn with pipe for stdin/stdout/stderr, no PTY
description: From Advanced Elicitation difficulty #6. Implementation approach: use Bun.spawn with stdin/stdout/stderr all set to 'pipe'. Read streams, forward to original destinations AND post to daemon. Simple pipe-based, no PTY emulation. If color issues arise, address later. Linked to ch-ek3.

title: User persona insights: power user wants filters, plugin dev wants wrap, casual user wants human-readable labels
description: From Advanced Elicitation User Persona Focus Group. Power user: filter panel matters more than event count. Plugin developer: v0 has limited value without wrap feature (v1). Casual user: web UI needs human-readable labels (e.g. 'Claude read this file' not 'PreToolUse'). No action required now — inform v0 and v1 UI design.

title: Upstream architecture: observability uses manual server start + HTTP POST from hooks
description: Research finding from Advanced Elicitation. The observability upstream uses: manual bun run dev to start server, Python hooks send HTTP POST to localhost:4000/events, WebSocket broadcast to Vue client, silent failure if server not running (events lost). hookwatch improves on this with auto-start daemon. Reference: .git-ignored/upstream/claude-code-hooks-multi-agent-observability/

title: Responsive column widths: shrink session ID column on narrow terminals

title: Two-line mode: no way to clear focus (deselect)
description: In large layout, Esc and header click both clear selection (set selected_index = None). In two-line mode, there is no header to click, and Esc behavior may not match. Users must click vertical bar separators to change focus but cannot fully deselect. Both modes should support clearing focus consistently. Related: gap 9 from acd-h15i audit, activation-gestures.md.

title: Two-line mode: auto-focus-back to CWD session after inactivity
description: In two-line mode (dashboard mode), accept a parameter for the 'home' session (by CWD). After the user navigates away and is inactive for N seconds, auto-focus back to that session. Needs design discussion: (1) threshold value (10s? 15s?), (2) performance impact of idle timer, (3) parameter naming — avoid collision with CWD env var. Candidates: --home-dir, --focus-dir, --session-cwd. This makes two-line mode useful as an ambient dashboard that always returns to the relevant session.

title: Scroll/focus inconsistency between large and two-line mode
description: In large layout mode, scroll wheel changes the focused session. In two-line mode, scroll navigates chips but does NOT visually change focus the same way — the focused chip bracket style updates but the behavior feels inconsistent with large mode. Both modes should have consistent scroll-to-focus behavior. Related: gap 8 from acd-h15i audit, activation-gestures.md.

title: Migrate post-merge hook to pre-merge-commit hook
description: Git supports pre-merge-commit (since Git 2.24) which runs BEFORE the merge commit is created. Currently we use post-merge which runs AFTER — meaning a bad merge commit lands on main and must be fixed afterward. With pre-merge-commit, if formatting or tests fail, the merge never commits, keeping main clean. Migrate scripts/post-merge.sh to scripts/pre-merge-commit.sh and update the symlink.

title: Uninstall should ask user about keeping config file
description: Currently acd uninstall silently preserves the config file and prints its path. Change: prompt the user 'Keep config file? [Y/n]' with default yes (preserve). This makes the behavior explicit instead of surprising. Also: we may be missing a CLI user reference doc — consider creating docs/user/cli-reference.md or similar. Related: gap 4 from acd-h15i audit.

title: Report TUI hook execution results to user
description: Currently TUI hooks (activate, reopen) run fire-and-forget on a background thread. Output is logged at debug level only. Users get no feedback if their hook fails. Change: show hook exit status and stderr in the TUI feedback overlay (status bar). Success: brief confirmation. Failure: show error message. Related: gap 2 from acd-h15i audit.

title: Update design doc: lead with outcomes, not event counts
description: Vision should lead with 'see what Claude did, debug your hooks, query your sessions' rather than '18 event types'. The count is infrastructure, not the value prop. Still mention 'all 18' but not as the headline.

Currently the Stop event does not fire on user interrupt (ESC/Ctrl+C) — confirmed by official docs: 'Does not run if the stoppage occurred due to a user interrupt.' This leaves ACD with no way to detect when a user cancels a prompt. Check existing issues (#13024 etc.) before filing to avoid duplicates. Related: acd-4cqb.

Currently we only detect MCP elicitation dialogs via Notification(elicitation_dialog). AskUserQuestion does NOT fire elicitation_dialog (confirmed: GitHub #13830, #20169). This is a separate event. Part of the hook redesign (replacing PreToolUse→working with PostToolUse→working).
